# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for postgres weak auth and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf - Production Configuration
# =============================================================
# This file controls client authentication for PostgreSQL.
# 
# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Local connections (Unix domain socket)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Allow the postgres superuser to connect locally for maintenance
# using peer authentication (OS user must match database user)
local   all             postgres                                peer

# Admin users authenticate via scram-sha-256 on local socket
local   all             admin                                   scram-sha-256

# Application user on local socket
local   app_database    app_user                                scram-sha-256

# Readonly user on local socket
local   app_database    readonly_user                           scram-sha-256

# Deny all other local connections explicitly
local   all             all                                     reject

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# IPv4 host-based connections (TCP/IP)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Loopback - admin with scram-sha-256
host    all             admin           127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256

# Application servers subnet - app_user only to app_database
# Using scram-sha-256 (strongest password-based auth) with SSL required
hostssl app_database    app_user        10.10.1.0/24            scram-sha-256

# Application servers subnet - readonly_user only to app_database
hostssl app_database    readonly_user   10.10.1.0/24            scram-sha-256

# Admin connections restricted to management subnet with SSL required
hostssl all             admin           10.10.0.0/28            scram-sha-256

# Monitoring server - readonly access only with SSL
hostssl app_database    readonly_user   10.10.2.10/32           scram-sha-256

# Replication connections from designated replica servers with SSL
hostssl replication     repl_user       10.10.3.0/29            scram-sha-256

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# IPv6 host-based connections (TCP/IP)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Loopback IPv6 - admin with scram-sha-256
host    all             admin           ::1/128                 scram-sha-256

# Deny all other IPv6 connections
host    all             all             ::0/0                   reject

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Reject everything else (catch-all deny rules)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Reject any IPv4 connection not matched above
host    all             all             0.0.0.0/0               reject

# Reject any SSL IPv4 connection not matched above
hostssl all             all             0.0.0.0/0               reject

# Reject non-SSL connections to application and admin subnets
# (forces SSL usage for remote connections)
hostnossl all           all             10.10.0.0/16            reject

# =============================================================
# SECURITY NOTES:
# =============================================================
# 1. scram-sha-256 is used exclusively for password-based auth.
#    - md5 is NOT used as it is vulnerable to pass-the-hash attacks.
#    - trust is NOT used anywhere (would allow passwordless access).
#    - password (cleartext) is NOT used anywhere.
#
# 2. peer authentication is used ONLY for the local postgres
#    superuser via Unix socket (OS-level identity verification).
#
# 3. All remote connections require SSL (hostssl) except the
#    loopback address. hostnossl rules explicitly reject non-SSL
#    connections from internal subnets.
#
# 4. Network ranges are as narrow as possible:
#    - /32 for single hosts (monitoring server)
#    - /29 for replication (max 6 usable hosts)
#    - /28 for admin management subnet (max 14 usable hosts)
#    - /24 for application servers
#
# 5. Each user role is restricted to specific databases:
#    - admin: all databases (management purposes)
#    - app_user: app_database only
#    - readonly_user: app_database only
#    - repl_user: replication only
#
# 6. Explicit reject rules at the end ensure deny-by-default.
#
# 7. Ensure postgresql.conf has:
#    - password_encryption = 'scram-sha-256'
#    - ssl = on
#    - ssl_cert_file, ssl_key_file, ssl_ca_file configured
#    - listen_addresses restricted appropriately
# =============================================================
